name: Natural Resources Conservation Serv
description_short: Federal credit union serving government employees in Fort Worth, TX since 1937. Offers checking, savings, and loan products with $6M in assets.
description_long:
Natural Resources Conservation Service Federal Credit Union is a federally chartered credit union headquartered in Fort Worth, Texas, with roots dating back to 1937—making it nearly 89 years old. The institution was established to serve members of federal, state, and local government agencies, reflecting its mission-driven origins in the public sector.

The credit union offers standard banking and financial services including share draft accounts (checking), regular savings accounts, ATM/debit card programs, and various loan products. Their loan portfolio includes unsecured loans, real estate loans, and other loan types. They maintain one branch location at their main office in Fort Worth and serve approximately 737 members with total assets of $6.01 million as of September 2025.

As a federal credit union with membership restricted to government employees, NRCS FCU distinguishes itself through its specialized membership base and long institutional history. The organization maintains low-cost wire transfer services and operates under federal credit union regulations, providing member-focused governance through a board structure led by Chairperson Cherie LaFleur and CEO Paul Withey.

However, prospective members should note that the credit union reported a net loss of $25,656 in its most recent financial period, despite positive loan and investment income. This deficit, combined with relatively modest asset size and single-branch footprint, suggests limited growth trajectory and potentially constrained service expansion. The organization is best suited for eligible government employees seeking basic banking services from an established, mission-driven institution rather than those requiring comprehensive financial services or competitive rates. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: All other share and deposit products
pros: Federal credit union status provides member protection through NCUSIF deposit insurance
Established 89-year history (since 1937) demonstrates institutional stability and longevity
Specialized membership serving federal, state, and local government employees
ATM/debit card program and multiple account types (share drafts, regular shares, other deposits)
Member-focused governance structure with elected board leadership
Smaller, community-oriented institution with personalized service approach
cons: Reported net loss of $25,656 in most recent financial period raises solvency concerns
Single branch location limits accessibility and convenience for members
Membership restricted to government employees—not open to general public
Modest asset base of $6M suggests limited lending capacity and service breadth
No investment products offered (zero total investments reported)
best_for: Federal, state, and local government employees in Fort Worth seeking basic banking
Government workers wanting member-owned credit union governance and stability
Employees of Natural Resources Conservation Service needing employee-affiliated banking
diagnosis: Natural Resources Conservation Service Federal Credit Union is best for eligible government employees in the Fort Worth area seeking basic, member-owned banking services from an established institution. The main caveat is the recent operating loss and modest asset base, which may indicate operational challenges and limited service expansion capacity compared to larger financial institutions.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
